# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

class Report {
    private $db;

    public function __construct(PDO $db) {
        $this->db = $db;
    }

    public function generateReport() {
        $sql = "SELECT id, status, category, date, report_data FROM reports WHERE 1=1";

        if (isset($_POST['status']) && !empty($_POST['status'])) {
            $status = $_POST['status'];
            $sql .= " AND status='$status'";
        }

        if (isset($_POST['category']) && !empty($_POST['category'])) {
            $category = $_POST['category'];
            $sql .= " AND category='$category'";
        }

        if (isset($_POST['start_date']) && isset($_POST['end_date'])) {
            $start_date = $_POST['start_date'];
            $end_date = $_POST['end_date'];
            $sql .= " AND date BETWEEN '$start_date' AND '$end_date'";
        }

        try {
            $stmt = $this->db->prepare($sql);
            $stmt->execute();
            return $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
        } catch (PDOException $e) {
            die("Error: " . $e->getMessage());
        }
    }
}
